driveParameter.title: Simply Start and Commissioning
driveParameter.description: Macro selection, basic motor nameplate entry, and first-start items used to commission the drive quickly.

driveParameter.title: Command and Reference
driveParameter.description: Command profile, channel switching, preset speeds, jog, reference switching, and reference operations.

driveParameter.title: Ramps and Stop Modes
driveParameter.description: Ramp shape, stop behavior, DC injection, fluxing, and deceleration behavior across normal and application stops.

driveParameter.title: Motor Control and Tuning
driveParameter.description: Motor model, auto-tuning, frequency range, encoder setup, and core drive-control behavior.

driveParameter.title: Logic I/O and Output Assignment
driveParameter.description: Logic inputs, relays, logic outputs, digital assignments, and the live digital image words behind them.

driveParameter.title: Analog I/O and Scaling
driveParameter.description: Analog inputs and outputs, pulse/frequency channels, scaling, and live analog signal images.

driveParameter.title: PID Regulation
driveParameter.description: Feedback assignment, scaling, gains, PID references, offsets, limits, and closed-loop behavior.

driveParameter.title: Process Flow and Damper Functions
driveParameter.description: Sleep and wake logic, process underload and overload supervision, no-flow detection, flow limitation, and damper control functions.

driveParameter.title: Torque and Current Control
driveParameter.description: Torque mode, torque limits, second current limit, torque reference handling, and related dynamic limits.

driveParameter.title: Brake, Contactors, and Power Path
driveParameter.description: Brake logic, contactors, resistor protection, DC bus supply, and regeneration path configuration.

driveParameter.title: Positioning and Traverse
driveParameter.description: Limit switches, positioning by sensors, traverse control, and motion features used in handling and textile machines.

driveParameter.title: Parameter Sets and Multimotor
driveParameter.description: Parameter set switching, set contents, and multi-motor configuration families.

driveParameter.title: Protection and Supervision
driveParameter.description: Thermal, undervoltage, phase, sensor, slip, and external-fault protection or supervision settings.

driveParameter.title: Faults, Histories, and Recovery
driveParameter.description: Current faults, fault snapshots, reset behavior, restart handling, and recovery-related diagnostics.

driveParameter.title: Controller Inside Card
driveParameter.description: Controller Inside registers and card-specific values exposed through the Modbus model.

driveParameter.title: Service, Factory, and Terminal
driveParameter.description: Factory config actions, card pairing, terminal behavior, test procedures, and other service-oriented controls.

driveParameter.title: Operational Values
driveParameter.description: Status words, references, actual values, measurements, CiA 402 values, and other live operating data.

driveParameter.title: Identification and Nameplate
driveParameter.description: Drive identity, firmware, and nameplate-style identification values useful for service and asset tracking.

driveParameter.title: General Settings and Thresholds
driveParameter.description: Remaining ATV61 settings, thresholds, and auxiliary controls that do not belong to a narrower function family.

communication.title: Communication Maps
communication.description: Command/status word maps, scanner images, and mapped addresses used by PLCs and higher-level controllers.

communication.title: Network and Fieldbus
communication.description: Integrated Modbus, CANopen, communication-card settings, forced local, and network-facing supervision parameters.

registers.name: Number of pairs of poles calculated
Logic outputs states
notes: bit 2: Value of R3, if a logic I/O extension card is installed.
bit 3: Value of R4, if an extended I/O extension card is installed.

The outputs (relays or logic ouputs) can be controled by the network. It is enough to write this parameter. The outputs to control should not be assigned to a function of the drive, otherwise the writing is without effect.

registers.name: Controller logic inputs states
notes: bit 0: LI51 logic input physical image
bit 1: LI52 logic input physical image
bit 2: LI53 logic input physical image
bit 3: LI54 logic input physical image
bit 4: LI55 logic input physical image
bit 5: LI56 logic input physical image
bit 6: LI57 logic input physical image
bit 7: LI58 logic input physical image
bit 8: LI59 logic input physical image
bit 9: LI60 logic input physical image

registers.name: Controller logic outputs states
notes: bit 8: LO51 logic output physical image
bit 9: LO52 logic output physical image
bit 10: LO53 logic output physical image
bit 11: LO54 logic output physical image
bit 12: LO55 logic output physical image
bit 13: LO56 logic output physical image

registers.name: Incorrect configuration
notes: bit 2: = 1: The network card has been removed.
bit 3: = 1: Saving to the EEPROM non-volatile memory is inconsistent with power on.
bit 4: = 1: The network card has been changed.
bit 6: = 1: The (Controller Inside) card has been removed.
bit 7: = 1: The (Controller Inside) card has been changed.
bit 9: = 1: An I/O card has been removed.
bit 10: = 1: The I/O card has been changed.
bit 12: = 1: An encoder card has been removed.
bit 13: = 1: The encoder card has been changed.
If one of these events occurs, the drive switches to [Incorrect config.] (CFF) fault mode and then automatically applies a factory setting.

registers.name: Status word on last fault
notes: Possible values in the IO profile
Note: The value is identical in CiA402 and I/O profiles. In I/O profile, the description of the values is simplified and does not refer to the CiA402 (Drivecom) state chart.
= 0: The drive will not start if a reference other than zero is applied.
= 1: Running, if a reference other than zero is applied, the drive can start.
bit 4: Power section line supply present
= 0: Command via the terminals or the graphic display terminal
= 0: The reference is within the limits
= 1: The reference is not within the limits

When the drive is in speed mode, the limits are defined by the "Low speed (LSP)" and "High speed (HSP)" parameters. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual).
= 1: Stop triggered by the STOP key on the graphic display terminal
bit 0: Ready to switch on, power section line supply pending
bit 4: Voltage enabled, power section line supply present
When the drive is powered by the power section only, this bit is always at 1.
bit 6: Switched on disabled, power section line supply locked.
bit 9: Remote, command or reference via the network
= 0: Command or reference via the terminals
= 1: Command or reference via the network

When the drive is in speed mode, this is the speed reference. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual). When the drive stops, the reference has been reached.
bit 11: Internal limit active, reference outside limits
= 0: The reference is within the limits
= 1: The reference is not within the limits

When the drive is in speed mode, the limits are defined by the "Low speed (LSP)" and "High speed (HSP)" parameters. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual).
bit 14: Stop key, STOP via stop key
= 1: Stop triggered by the STOP key on the graphic display terminal

registers.name: Extended status word on last fault
notes: bit 0: = 1: Access to the EEPROM non-volatile memory in progress
bit 2: = 0: The drive is not in fault state or a fault is present
= 1: The drive is in fault state but the fault is no longer present (not reset)
bit 4: = 1: The drive is in speed mode
bit 5: = 1: DC injection braking (identical to LSR4, bit 11)
bit 7: = 1: Motor thermal state threshold reached for the active motor
bit 9: = 1: Acceleration in progress (identical to LSR4, bit 13)
bit 10: = 1: Deceleration in progress (identical to LSR4, bit 14)
bit 11: = 1: Current or torque limiting in progress
bit 12: = 1: Fast stop in progress (identical to LSR4, bit 15)
bit 13: bit 13 = 0 and bit 14 = 0: Command via the terminals
bit 14: bit 13 = 1 and bit 14 = 0: Command via the graphic display terminal
bit 13 = 0 and bit 14 = 1: Command via Modbus
bit 13 = 1 and bit 14 = 1: Command via CANopen, the network card or the "Controller Inside" card
bit 15: = 0: Forward operation applied before the ramp
= 1: Reverse operation applied before the ramp

registers.name: Command word on last fault
notes: The assignment of bit 0 cannot be modified. It corresponds to the assignment of the terminals. It can be switched.
Bit 0 (Cd00) is only active if the channel of this control word is active.
= 1: Run is authorized, on a forward or reverse command.
bit 1: Forward (on 0 V 1 rising edge) command

The assignment of bits 0 and 1 cannot be modified. It corresponds to the assignment of the terminals. It can be switched. Bits 0 (Cd00) and 1 (Cd01) are only active if the channel of this control word is active.
Bits 2 to 15 can be assigned to commands.
Possible values in CiA402 profile, separate or not separate mode:
bit 1: Disable voltage/Authorization to supply AC power
bit 7: Fault reset/Fault acknowledgment active on 0 V 1 rising edge
= 1: Reverse rotation. Default value, this bit can be assigned to another command.
bit 12: Can be assigned to a command.
bit 13: Can be assigned to a command.
bit 14: Can be assigned to a command.
bit 15: Can be assigned to a command.

registers.name: Motor operating time on last fault
Power supply voltage on last fault
Motor thermal state on last fault
Channels active on last fault
notes: 0 = The terminals are the active control channel.
2 = The graphic display terminal is the active control channel.
3 = Modbus is the active control channel.
6 = CANopen is the active control channel.
7 = The terminals are the active channel in the +/- speed reference.
8 = The graphic display terminal is the active channel (up-down speed)
9 = The network card is the active control channel.
10 = The "Controller Inside" card is the active control channel.
15 = The PowerSuite software workshop is the active control channel.
0 = The terminals are the active channel via an analog input.
2 = The graphic display terminal is the active channel.
7 = The terminals are the active channel in the +/- speed reference.
8 = The graphic display terminal is the active channel (up-down speed)
9 = The network card is the active channel.
10 = The "Controller Inside" card is the active channel.
15 = The PowerSuite software workshop is the active channel.

registers.name: Active command channel on fault n-4

Status word on fault n-5
notes: Possible values in the IO profile
Note: The value is identical in CiA402 and I/O profiles. In I/O profile, the description of the values is simplified and does not refer to the CiA402 (Drivecom) state chart.
= 0: The drive will not start if a reference other than zero is applied.
= 1: Running, if a reference other than zero is applied, the drive can start.
bit 4: Power section line supply present
= 0: Command via the terminals or the graphic display terminal
= 0: The reference is within the limits
= 1: The reference is not within the limits

= 1: Stop triggered by the STOP key on the graphic display terminal
bit 0: Ready to switch on, power section line supply pending
bit 4: Voltage enabled, power section line supply present
When the drive is powered by the power section only, this bit is always at 1.
bit 6: Switched on disabled, power section line supply locked.
bit 9: Remote, command or reference via the network
= 0: Command or reference via the terminals
= 1: Command or reference via the network

When the drive is in speed mode, this is the speed reference. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual). When the drive stops, the reference has been reached.
bit 11: Internal limit active, reference outside limits
= 0: The reference is within the limits
= 1: The reference is not within the limits

When the drive is in speed mode, the limits are defined by the "Low speed (LSP)" and "High speed (HSP)" parameters. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual).
bit 14: Stop key, STOP via stop key
= 1: Stop triggered by the STOP key on the graphic display terminal

notes: bit 0: = 1: Access to the EEPROM non-volatile memory in progress

bit 13 = 1 and bit 14 = 1: Command via CANopen, the network card or the "Controller Inside" card
bit 15: = 0: Forward operation applied before the ramp
= 1: Reverse operation applied before the ramp

registers.name: Active command channel on fault n-8
Active reference channel on fault n-8
Factory setting group
notes: Selection of the menus to download.
bit 1 : =1: [Drive menu] (drM : menu [1 DRIVE MENU] without [1.9 COMMUNICATION] nor [1.14 CONTROL. INSIDE CARD ]. In menu [7 DISPLAY CONFIG.], [Return std name] go to [No].
bit 2 : =1: [Settings] (SEt): menu [1.3 SETTINGS] without parameters [IR compensation] (UFr), [Slip compensation] (SLP) and [Mot. therm. current] (ItH)

[Rated motor power] (nPr) - [Rated motor volt.] (UnS) - [Rated mot. current] (nCr) - [Rated motor freq.] (FrS) - [Rated motor speed] (nSP) - [Auto-tuning] (tUn) - [Auto tuning state] (tUS) - [U0] (U0) to [U5] (U5) - [F1] (F1) to [F5] (F5) - [V. constant power] (UCP) - [Freq. Const Power] (FCP) - [Nominal I sync.] (nCrS) - [Nom motor spdsync.] (nSPS) - [Pole pairs] (PPnS) - [Syn. EMF constant] (PHS) - [Autotune L d-axis] (LdS) - [Autotune L q-axis] (LqS) - [Cust. stator R syn] (rSAS) - [IR compensation] (UFr) - [Slip compensation] (SLP) - motor parameters accessibles in [Expert] level.
The following selection is accessible only if [Config. Source] (FCSI) = [Macro-Conf] (InI) :
bit 4 : =1: [Comm. menu] (COM): menu [1.9 COMMUNICATION] without [Scan. IN1 address] (nMA1) to [Scan. IN8 address] (nMA8) and [Scan.Out1 address] (nCA1) to [Scan.Out8 address] (nCA8).
bit 5 : =1: [Control. insid. menu] (PLC): menu [1.14 CONTROL. INSIDE CARD ].

bit 9: = 1: Acceleration in progress (identical to LSR4, bit 13)
bit 10: = 1: Deceleration in progress (identical to LSR4, bit 14)
bit 11: = 1: Current or torque limiting in progress
bit 12: = 1: Fast stop in progress (identical to LSR4, bit 15)
bit 13: bit 13 = 0 and bit 14 = 0: Command via the terminals
bit 14: bit 13 = 1 and bit 14 = 0: Command via the graphic display terminal
bit 13 = 0 and bit 14 = 1: Command via Modbus
bit 13 = 1 and bit 14 = 1: Command via CANopen, the network card or the "Controller Inside" card
bit 15: = 0: Forward operation applied before the ramp
= 1: Reverse operation applied before the ramp

notes: bit 0: = 1: The terminals are the active channel via an analog input.
bit 2: = 1: The graphic display terminal is the active channel.
bit 3: = 1: Modbus is the active channel.
bit 6: = 1: CANopen is the active channel.
bit 7: = 1: The terminals are the active channel in the +/- speed reference.

notes: bit 0: = 1: The terminals are the active control channel.
bit 2: = 1: The graphic display terminal is the active control channel.
bit 3: = 1: Modbus is the active control channel.
bit 6: = 1: CANopen is the active control channel.
bit 7: = 1: The terminals are the active channel in the +/- speed reference.
bit 8: = 1: The graphic display terminal is the active channel (up-down speed)
bit 9: = 1: The network card is the active control channel.
bit 10: = 1: The "Controller Inside" card is the active control channel.
bit 15: = 1: The PowerSuite software workshop is the active control channel.

registers.name: Extended control word
notes: bit 1: Save configuration to EEPROM non-volatile memory command (active at 1).
This bit automatically changes to 0 after the request is taken into account. The command is only active if the drive is stopped, and not in "5-Operation enabled" state.
Note: If CMI is a periodic network variable, the PLC program must write it to 0 after the first request is taken into account. The life of the EEPROM memory is limited to 100,000 write operations.
Note: If the motor or configuration switching function is active, the configuration in the RAM is saved to the EEPROM in the configuration designated by [Config. Active] (CnFS).
bit 2: Restore configuration to EEPROM non-volatile memory command (active on 0 to 1 rising edge).
This bit automatically changes to 0 after the request is taken into account.
The command is only active if the drive is stopped, and not in "5-Operation enabled" state.
Note: If CMI is a periodic network variable, the PLC program must write it to 0 after the first request is taken into account.
This does not adversely affect the life of the EEPROM memory, but permanently copies the configuration in the memory to the current configuration.
Note: If the motor or configuration switching function is active, the configuration in the EEPROM designated by [Config. Active] (CnFS) is copied to the RAM.
bit 9: Definition of the frequency reference (LFr) and output frequency (rFr) unit:

= 1: 'Standardized value 16 signed bits based on the maximum frequency. The value 32767 corresponds to [Max frequency] (tFr). The default value of [Max frequency] (tFr) is 60 Hz, and the resolution is then approximately 0.0018 Hz.
This function has no effect on the speed reference (LFrd) or the output speed (rFrd).
bit 11: DC injection braking command (active at 1)

= 0: 'The check is activated. Each time a parameter is written, the drive checks the relationship between the written parameter and the configuration in the drive. For example, the [High speed] (HSP) parameter must be less than the [Max frequency] (tFr) parameter. If an attempt is made to write a value greater than the [Max frequency] (tFr) parameter to the [High speed] (HSP) parameter, the write operation is accepted, but the value is limited to that of [Max frequency] (tFr).

= 1: 'The check is deactivated. The drive is locked in stop mode. In this drive state, the configuration can be written parameter by parameter and the drive does not modify the values that are written.
The switch from 1 to 0 triggers a calculation of the consistency of the configuration. Some parameters can be modified automatically by the drive.

registers.name: Extended status word 1
notes: bit 1: = 1: The drive is in fault state.
bit 2: = 0: The drive is locked, the motor is not powered
= 1: The drive is unlocked, power can be supplied to the motor (RUN state)
bit 3: = 1: The output contactor is controlled
bit 4: = 1: Frequency threshold (ftd) reached: [Freq. Th. attained] (FtA)
bit 6: = 1: Current threshold (Ctd) reached: [Current Th. attained] (CtA)
bit 7: = 1: Frequency reference reached: [Frequency ref. att.] (SrA)
bit 8: = 1: Motor 1 thermal state threshold [Motor therm. level] (ttd) reached: [Motor th. state att.] (tSA)
bit 10: = 1: PID regulator error alarm: [PID error al] (PEE)
bit 11: = 1: PID regulator feedback alarm: [PID fdbk al.] (PFA)
bit 12: = 1: 4-20 mA alarm on analog input AI2: [AI2 4-20 mA loss] (LFF2)
bit 13: = 1: Second frequency threshold (ftd) reached: [Freq. Th. 2 attained] (FA2)
bit 14: = 1: Drive thermal state threshold [Drv therm. state al] (tHA) reached: [Th. drv. att.] (tAd)

registers.name: Extended status word 2
notes: bit 0 : =1 : [High Ref.] (rtAH): Frequency reference is greater than the threshold [High Freq. Ref. Thr.] (rtd)
bit 1 : =1 : [Low Ref.] (rtAL): Frequency reference is less than the threshold [Low Freq. Ref. Thr.] (rtdL)
bit 2 : =1 : [Low F.Thd.] (FtAL): Frequency is less than the threshold [Low Freq.Threshold] (FtdL)
bit 3 : =1 : [2Low F.Thld] (F2AL): Frequency is less than the threshold [2 Freq. Threshold] (F2dL)
bit 4 : =1 : [Low I Thres. Attain.] (CtAL): Current is less than the threshold [Low I Threshold] (CtdL)
bit 7 : =1 : [PID high Al.] (PFAH): PID feedback alarm greater than [Max fbk alarm] (PAH)
bit 8 : =1 : [PID low Al.] (PFAL): PID feedback alarm less than [Min fbk alarm] (PAL)
bit 9 : =1 : [Regul.Alarm] (PISH): PID regulator fault (cannot reach reference)
bit 10 : =1 : [Emerg. Run] (Ern): The drive is in emergency run mode

registers.name: Extended status word 3
notes: bit 0: = 0: Reference channel 1 or 1B (Fr1) or (Fr1b) is active
bit 5: = 1: Motor 2 thermal state threshold [Motor2 therm. level] (ttd2) reached: [Th.mot2 att] (tS2)
bit 6: = 1: Motor 3 thermal state threshold [Motor3 therm. level] (ttd3) reached: [Th.mot3 att] (tS3)
bit 8: =1 : 24 VDC external power supply present
bit 9: =1 : Stop on low speed time limit function [Low speed time out] (tLS)
bit 10: =1 : [Cd distance] (bMP) : La commande par le terminal graphique est activée par une touche fonction de ce terminal graphique.
bit 15: = 0: The output torque is positive (forward)

registers.name: Extended status word 4
notes: bit 4: = 1: Parameter set 1 is active: [Set 1 active] (CFP1)
bit 5: = 1: Parameter set 2 is active: [Set 2 active] (CFP2)
bit 6: = 1: Parameter set 3 is active: [Set 3 active] (CFP3)
bit 8: = 0: Power section line supply present
bit 9: = 1: Motor "fluxing" in progress: [In motor fluxing] (FLX)
bit 11: = 1: DC injection braking (identical to ETI, bit 5)
bit 13: = 1: Acceleration in progress (identical to ETI, bit 9)
bit 14: = 1: Deceleration in progress (identical to ETI, bit 10)
bit 15: = 1: Fast stop in progress: [Fast stop in prog.] (FSt) (identical to ETI, bit 12)

registers.name: Extended status word 5
notes: bit 0: = 1: Drive DC bus loading: [DC bus loading] (dbL)
bit 2: = 1: The "Power removal" function is active
bit 3: = 1: Automatic restart attempts in progress: [Auto restart] (AUtO)
bit 5: = 1: Controlled stop in progress following loss of power section line supply (CTL)
bit 6: = 1: The drive cannot follow the configured deceleration ramp, deceleration automatically adapted (OBR)
bit 7: = 1: Controlled output cut in progress (SOC)
bit 8 : = 1: [Freq. meter Alarm] (FqLA): Measured speed threshold reached: [Pulse warning thd.] (FqL).
bit 9: = 1: The line contactor is active
bit 13: = 1: Current present in the motor (MCP)

registers.name: Extended status word 6
notes: bit 5: = 1: LI6 PTC probe alarm: [LI6 =PTC alarm] (PtC3)
bit 15: = 1: Current or torque limit alarm after time-out [Trq/I limit. time out] (StO)

registers.name: Extended status word 7
notes: bit 0: = 1: Reference channel 1 or 1B (Fr1) or (Fr1b) is active
bit 10: = 1: Alarm sent by the "Controller Inside" card
bit 11: = 1: 4-20 mA alarm on analog input AI3: [AI3 4-20 mA loss] (LFF3)
bit 12: = 1: 4-20 mA alarm on analog input AI4: [AI4 4-20 mA loss] (LFF4)
bit 13: DC bus precharging contactor controlled (DC0)
bit 14: =1 : [En lim.débit] (FSA) : Limitation de débit active.

registers.name: Units of parameters APH, PTH and RTH
notes: The unit changes automatically when the value reached the maximum format of the parameter.
bit 0 + bit 1 = unit of APH.: 0 = Wh, 1 = kWh, 2 = MWh
bit 2 + bit 3 = unit of PTH.: 0 = seconds, 1 = minutes, 2 = hours
bit 4 + bit 5 = unit of RTH.: 0 = seconds, 1 = minutes, 2 = hours
bit 6 + bit 7 = unit of RTHI.: 0 = seconds, 1 = minutes, 2 = hours
bit 8 + bit 9 = unit of IPHR.: 0 = Wh, 1 = kWh, 2 = MWh
bit 10 + bit 11 = unit of IPH.: 0 = Wh, 1 = kWh, 2 = MWh

registers.name: Status word
notes: Possible values in the IO profile
Note: The value is identical in CiA402 and I/O profiles. In I/O profile, the description of the values is simplified and does not refer to the CiA402 (Drivecom) state chart.
= 0: The drive will not start if a reference other than zero is applied.
= 1: Running, if a reference other than zero is applied, the drive can start.
bit 4: Power section line supply present
= 0: Command via the terminals or the graphic display terminal
= 0: The reference is within the limits
= 1: The reference is not within the limits

When the drive is in speed mode, the limits are defined by the "Low speed (LSP)" and "High speed (HSP)" parameters. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual).
= 1: Stop triggered by the STOP key on the graphic display terminal
bit 0: Ready to switch on, power section line supply pending
bit 4: Voltage enabled, power section line supply present
When the drive is powered by the power section only, this bit is always at 1.
bit 6: Switched on disabled, power section line supply locked.
bit 9: Remote, command or reference via the network
= 0: Command or reference via the terminals
= 1: Command or reference via the network

When the drive is in speed mode, this is the speed reference. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual). When the drive stops, the reference has been reached.
bit 11: Internal limit active, reference outside limits
= 0: The reference is within the limits
= 1: The reference is not within the limits

When the drive is in speed mode, the limits are defined by the "Low speed (LSP)" and "High speed (HSP)" parameters. When the torque function is activated, refer to the description of this function (see the programming manual).
bit 14: Stop key, STOP via stop key
= 1: Stop triggered by the STOP key on the graphic display terminal

registers.name: Control word
notes: The assignment of bit 0 cannot be modified. It corresponds to the assignment of the terminals. It can be switched.
Bit 0 (Cd00) is only active if the channel of this control word is active.
= 1: Run is authorized, on a forward or reverse command.
bit 1: Forward (on 0 V 1 rising edge) command

The assignment of bits 0 and 1 cannot be modified. It corresponds to the assignment of the terminals. It can be switched. Bits 0 (Cd00) and 1 (Cd01) are only active if the channel of this control word is active.
Bits 2 to 15 can be assigned to commands.
Possible values in CiA402 profile, separate or not separate mode:
bit 1: Disable voltage/Authorization to supply AC power
bit 7: Fault reset/Fault acknowledgment active on 0 V 1 rising edge
= 1: Reverse rotation. Default value, this bit can be assigned to another command.
bit 12: Can be assigned to a command.
bit 13: Can be assigned to a command.
bit 14: Can be assigned to a command.
bit 15: Can be assigned to a command.
